title: Bulk Carrier Attacked by Multiple Small Craft Near Strait of Hormuz
summary: UKMTO reported a bulk carrier attacked by multiple small craft off Sirik, Iran, near the Strait of Hormuz. All crew reported safe. The attack marks at least two dozen incidents in and around the strait since the Iran war began.
contents:
## Bulk Carrier Attack Near Strait of Hormuz

### Incident Details
The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) reported on May 3, 2026 that a northbound bulk carrier sustained an attack by multiple small craft approximately 11 nautical miles west of Sirik, Iran, near the Strait of Hormuz.

### Crew and Environmental Status
All crew members aboard the unidentified vessel reported safe following the incident. UKMTO confirmed no environmental impact from the attack. The vessel continued its transit after the encounter.

### Regional Context
This attack represents at least the two dozen incident in and around the Strait of Hormuz since the US-Israel war on Iran commenced on February 28, 2026. Iranian patrol boats, some powered by twin outboard motors, have conducted numerous attacks on commercial shipping. President Trump ordered the US military to "shoot and kill" small Iranian boats deploying mines in the strait.

### Maritime Security Implications
The incident underscores the persistent threat to commercial navigation through one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints. The UKMTO maintains a "critical" threat level for the area. Iran has asserted control over the strait, demanding tolls from non-US, non-Israeli vessels seeking passage. US Central Command (CENTCOM) reported redirecting 49 commercial vessels under its counter-blockade enforcement on the same day.

### Source Attribution
- UKMTO official alert
- NBC News / Associated Press reporting
- Al Jazeera confirmation
